Text: AI coding agents need more than a model call. They need operating rules.

Claude Code Prompts is an independently authored prompt collection for builders designing AI coding agents, with templates for system behavior, tools, subagents, memory, and multi-agent coordination.

It helps you design an agent workflow faster by giving you scan-friendly patterns you can copy, adapt, and replace with your own stack, tool names, and risk policy.

Key features:

• System prompt template – covers agent identity, permissions, code style, safety, tool routing, tone, and output efficiency
• Tool prompt library – 11 prompts for shell, file read/edit/write, grep/glob search, web search/fetch, agent launch, ask-user, and plan mode
• Subagent prompts – templates for general research, code exploration, architecture, verification, and documentation tasks
• Memory + coordination prompts – session summaries, memory extraction, memory consolidation, and a coordinator workflow
• Pattern analyses + Cursor skills – 9 pattern breakdowns plus 3 drop-in Cursor skills for standards, verification, and prompt design

It’s open-source (MIT license).

Link in the reply 👇
